updates.tweet_text: Very simple session today in #ES_F and there's 7472 tagged again. This weeks plan was simple: Bulls had to hold 7368 (they did Friday), then reclaim 7421. We got another 7421 reclaim at 10:20AM. Targets were 7439, 7453, 7472 (hit). 7492 next, then we head to 7530. Dip, then 7590 https://t.co/fYFCdVqvU1
updates.notes: 7590 named as next bull target after 7530 — not in morning plan. All other prices (7421,7439,7453,7472,7492,7530) are plan levels or observations of hits.

updates.tweet_text:

7511 next, then we see 7530 which backtests Monday's breakdown. Dip, then 7590 https://t.co/fPIofvvPCU

updates.tweet_text:
Holiday week so volatility in #ES_F is dropping to 0. Don't trade unless we get a big "elevator down" sell. Last long was the 10:20AM 7421 Reclaim yesterday. My targets were 7492 (hit), 7511 (hit), 7530 (this is a magnet).

Ride your runner. 92, 82 (watch traps)=supports https://t.co/eb7Yt2eKvo
updates.notes: No new levels: 7492/7530 are morning-plan levels; 7511 is an observation; '92,82' are vague shorthand supports with no explicit actionable naming language; rest is trade management/commentary.

updates.tweet_text: @Porterhous90889 Might be abit too zoomed in! In ES a 50-70 elevator down is normal volatility. Good volatility is 100+ point elevator down, then excellent volatility I'd consider when we are easily making 150 point moves.
updates.notes: Pure educational commentary on volatility ranges (50-70, 100+, 150pts). No actionable price levels named, just distance/magnitude discussion.

updates.tweet_text:
Pinpoint defense of 7493 support. Followers here know that since last Monday, I've had one clear target in #ES_F that I've called a magnet: 7530. We are almost here. 7421 reclaim was last long trigger and we are +100 on that.

Once 7530 hits, we can head to 7544, 7560, then 7590 https://t.co/xzB5wexuJm
updates.notes: 7544 and 7560 named as sequential upside targets beyond 7530; not in morning plan. 7530/7590 are morning-plan levels; 7493/7421 are observations.

updates.tweet_text: Note: I have been posting this 7530 target multiple times a day for a week. It is charting 101: Whenever a major shelf breaks down (7530 last Monday), in ES it backtests 95-100% of the time. https://t.co/Wp5rLT86wg
updates.notes: 7530 is already in morning plan; tweet is educational hindsight commentary on a known level, not a new actionable signal.

updates.tweet_text:
There it is: 7530 hit in #ES_F. I have called for this target everyday since last Monday. It was a technical *requirement* it hit, and we did. Last long trigger was the 7421 reclaim to get us here. +100 on that.

Now, we ride that runner. 7544, 7560, then 7590 next. 90 new magnet https://t.co/o6G9bqhR7p
updates.notes: 7544 and 7560 named as next sequential bull targets ('7544, 7560, then 7590 next'); 7590 maps to morning-plan 7588, excluded. 7530 is morning plan.
